UK borrowing surges as public finances called 'fragile' -- The UK borrowed **£23.3bn** in May, up almost a third on the same month last year, with borrowing **£5.6bn** higher than

The UK's information commissioner John Edwards resigned after a workplace investigation concluded "there was a case to answer" and that his behaviour "fell short of the conduct expected from a public official"; Science Secretary Liz Kendall said she had seen evidence of "vulgar and highly sexualised language" used in interactions with staff.

The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails, with cybersecurity researchers signing an open letter calling the move dangerous.
Scottish Conservatives won the Aberdeen South Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years, marking the first such Westminster seat gain for the party in Scotland in that period.

The BBC was given rare access to Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon as part of a humanitarian convoy distributing aid to Christian villages, where mainly Shia villages have been completely destroyed by Israeli air strikes or demolitions, with human rights groups saying some of what happened there amounts to the deliberate destruction of civilian infrastructure.

Lebanese conservationist Mona Khalil, 76, died on Friday after an Israeli airstrike struck her beachside home two weeks ago near the city of Tyre; she was credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon that protected sea turtle nesting grounds across the Mediterranean coast.

Lloyds Banking Group has launched an AI recruitment drive for 300 tech experts to work on agentic AI by September, with the bank's generative AI programme already delivering a £50m boost to its balance sheet last year and a £100m benefit expected this year.

A new report by gender justice organisation Chayn warns that social media companies and authorities are failing women by focusing on nudity rather than consent when dealing with image-based abuse, arguing that a fully clothed image can have consequences as devastating as an intimate photograph in many conservative communities.

Keir Starmer has announced he is resigning as leader of the Labour Party and will leave office within weeks - about two years after his landslide election victory - with Andy Burnham, the outgoing mayor of Manchester, viewed as his likely successor.

Colombia's leftist presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda has vowed to challenge the results of the presidential runoff after Trump-backed far-right candidate Abelardo De La Espriella appeared headed for a narrow victory.
